LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TONY HSAIO TANG TU DOING BUSINESS AS H.T. CONSTRUCTION CO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD/FOURTH, SIXTH AND EIGHT CAUSES OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE SEVENTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND THE THIRD/FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION TO CORRECT THE TYPO THAT LISTS THE FIRST PAGE OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION AS THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION AND THE SECOND PAGE AS THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND TO SPECIFICALLY ALLEGE WHAT FRAUDULENT STATEMENTS DEFENDANT MADE, WHEN THEY WERE MADE, WHO THEY WERE MADE TO, AND HOW PLAINTIFF RELIED ON THE STATEMENTS. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION TO CORRECT THE VIOLATION OF THE THIRTY DAY NOTICE REQUIREMENT PURSUANT TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 1782. THE SEVENTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE EITHER ADEQUATELY PLED AND/OR THE DEMURRER TO THEM SERVES NO USEFUL PURPOSE IN ADVANCING THIS LITIGATION. ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO SEEK TREBLE DAMAGES PURSUANT TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 3345 BUT IT MUST BE AT THE TIME THEY ARE TETHERED TO A PARTICULAR CAUSE OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TONY HSAIO TANG TU, DOING BUSINESS AS H.T. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED IN PART. PAGE SIXTEEN (THE EXEMPLARY DAMAGES ATTACHMENT), THE WORDS "IMMACULATE CONSTRUCTION" IN PARAGRAPH FIVE ON PAGE TWENTY-FOUR, PARAGRAPH SIX ON PAGE TWENTY-FOUR, AND PARAGRAPH NINE ON PAGE TWENTY-FIVE ARE STRICKEN. ALL OTHER PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN ARE ADEQUATELY PLED AND THUS THE MOTION TO STRIKE THOSE PORTIONS ARE DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Augment Expert Witness Information
Off Calendar Jan-21-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

There being no appearance nor objection, the Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TONY HSAIO TANG TU'S Motion To Augment Expert Witness Information is Denied. Moving party defendant Tu has not satisfied the requirements of CCP sec. 2034.620. The need for the proposed new experts should have been anticipated by Tu and the new experts disclosed, at the latest, in a supplemental disclosure. The Court also notes there appears to be almost no effort to meet and confer made by Tu before filing this motion. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. While the Court would be disinclined to award sanctions on this motion in any event, the plaintiff's request is not clearly made and is unsubstantiated by calculation of a dollar amount in a declaration. Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-22-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Charlie W. Yu, Esq., Law Offices, 415-349-5180, for defendant and moving party
No appearance by Plaintiff

Discussed and adopted its tentative ruling as modified.
DEFENDANTS TONY HSAIO TANG TU DOING BUSINESS AS H.T. CONSTRUCTION CO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the third/fourth, sixth and eight causes of action and overruled as to the seventh and ninth causes of action. Plaintiff has leave to amend the third/fourth cause of action to correct the typo that lists the first page of the cause of action as the third cause of action and the second page as the fourth cause of action and to specifically allege what fraudulent statements Defendant made, when they were made, who they were made to, and how Plaintiff relied on the statements. Plaintiff has leave to amend the sixth cause of action to correct the violation of the thirty day notice requirement pursuant to Civil Code section 1782. The seventh and ninth causes of action are either adequately pled and/or the demurrer to them serves no useful purpose in advancing this litigation.
Additionally, Plaintiff has leave to amend to seek treble damages pursuant to Civil Code section 3345 but it must be at the time they are tethered to a particular cause of action.
Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
